weather_report:
Subiaco opens under a pewter sky, the morning light thinned to a soft glaze. A brush of light rain is due around late morning before the day settles into a steady quilt of overcast cloud. Sunshine will be scarce, more a suggestion than a fact; UV stays on the lower side but can nudge to moderate near midday when the cloud thins, so a dab of sunscreen won’t be wasted. The air feels rinsed and clear, edges softened, colors deepening on wet pavement.

Dress in easy layers with a light rain jacket and shoes that don’t mind puddles; an umbrella earns its keep for the lunchtime dash. It’s pleasant in a quiet, contemplative way—good weather for unhurried errands, a gallery stop, or a long coffee while the street steams and cools in turns. If you crave motion, take a short stroll between showers through the park and listen to the softened city; if not, let the afternoon be about simple tasks, tidy victories, and coming home dry.
